MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-17-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-17-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PURSUANT TO CCP 473(D), THE COURT MAY SET ASIDE ANY VOID JUDGMENT. LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION RENDERS A DEFAULT JUDGMENT VOID, SO THAT IT MAY BE VACATED AT ANY TIME. (STRATHVALE HOLDINGS V. E.B.H. (2005) 126 CAL.APP.4TH 1241, 1249.) BECAUSE DEFENDANT WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED AND HAS NOT GENERALLY APPEARED, THE COURT LACKS PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS VOID. (SEE CCP 410.50(A).) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A PENALTY AGAINST PLAINTIFF UNDER CCP 473(C)(1)(A) IS DENIED. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR ANY SUCH PENALTY HERE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. THE BURDEN OF PROOF IS ON THE PLAINTIFF TO PROVE THE FACTS REQUISITE TO AN EFFECTIVE SERVICE. (SUMMERS V. MCCLANAHAN (2006) 140 CAL.APP.4TH 403, 313.) ALTHOUGH EVIDENCE CODE 647 CREATES A PRESUMPTION THAT THE FACTS CONTAINED WITHIN A PROCESS SERVER'S AFFIDAVIT ARE TRUE, DEFENDANT REBUTTED THIS PRESUMPTION BY PROVIDING EVIDENCE THAT NO ONE WAS HOME WHEN THE SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ALLEGEDLY OCCURRED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF PROOF IN SHOWING THAT SUBSTITUTE SERVICE WAS PROPERLY EFFECTUATED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 (302)

Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Oct-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JACOB HARKER, ESQ., 415.624.7602, FOR DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons IS Granted. The burden of proof is on the plaintiff to prove the facts requisite to an effective service. (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 313.) Although Evidence Code 647 creates a presumption that the facts contained within a process server's affidavit are true, Defendant rebutted this presumption by providing evidence that no one was home when the substitute service allegedly occurred. Plaintiff has not met its burden of proof in showing that substitute service was properly effectuated. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JACOB HARKER, ESQ., 415.624.7602, FOR DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS Granted. Pursuant to CCP 473(d), the Court may set aside any void judgment. Lack of personal jurisdiction renders a default judgment void, so that it may be vacated at any time. (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1249.) Because Defendant was not properly served and has not generally appeared, the Court lacks personal jurisdiction and the default judgment is void. (See CCP 410.50(a).) Defendant's request for a penalty against Plaintiff under CCP 473(c)(1)(A) is denied. There is no basis for any such penalty here. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Continued To Oct-17-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is continued to October 17, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 18, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
